characters.name: Bill
characters.description: 1975 Alive Unknown Compare to his wife - Nancy, Bill is more chill and light-hearted. He loves his nephews Brady and Boomer. Unlike Nancy, who worries about them, Bill believes that they are doing fine and there is nothing to care about them.
characters.personality_section: Compare to his wife - Nancy, Bill is more chill and light-hearted. He loves his nephews Brady and Boomer. Unlike Nancy, who worries about them, Bill believes that they are doing fine and there is nothing to care about them.

characters.name: Boomer Parker
characters.description: King Maverick "Boomer" Parkeris the deuteragonist of first two seasons and the main protagonist in the third season ofPair of Kings. He isBozandBrady Parker's triplet-brother. He is one of the kings ofKinkow. He is portrayed byDoc Shaw. Boomer grew up inChicago, along with his twin brotherBrady. The two were raised by theirAunt NancyandUncle Bill. After being found hung on lockers, Boomer andBradywere told they were to inherit and rule the island ofKinkow. Although they thought it was joke at first, they went to the island to be crowned kings. (Return of the Kings) Boomer is very caring but is sometimes is selfish because he was ready to pack up and leave the island ofKinkowimmediately in a time of major crisis. He considers himself to be very good-looking, although he is not the smartest. He used to think of himself as a  good singer, until the episodeTone Deaf Jam, where he realizes that he can't sing.
characters.personality_section: Boomer is very caring but is sometimes is selfish because he was ready to pack up and leave the island ofKinkowimmediately in a time of major crisis. characters.name: Boz Parker
characters.description: Alive Co-king of KinkowKing of Mindu Human Boz was found and raised by a family of apes and taught their ways, hence his ape-like abilities, such as swinging through trees by using vines, climbing up ceilings, doing things with his feet such as eating and playing the violin. He's also a hugger, as all Minduians are. Having been a lost child and grown up in the wild, he shows sympathy for abandoned children and even brought back a Tarantula kid he thought was abandoned when he found him in the Dark Side and intended to raise him. Although he has his wild side, often seen screaming and screeching like a monkey when he's upset, Boz is also extremely trusting (he forgave his hated ex-girlfriend almost immediately when she apologized to him) and is very clingy to his new brother Boomer, and easily feels attached to him. After learning the ten bro-mandments, he gets upset when Boomer seemingly disrespects "[their] brotherhood" in episode two, and shows disdain toward Boomer's girlfriend Rebecca when his brother spends more time with her then him on their birthday. Boz is shown to also have a very vulnerable side, he gets upset and cries easily. He's naive and easily confused and often seen following Boomer's ideas, he showns to be even most stupider than Boomer, showing at very short intelligence, if not over, Being that of Brady’s two goofy younger brothers, Boz is even worst, calling out Boomer's dependence on his royal lifestyle on more than one occasion. Despite this, Boz prefers to think and act like a monkey, making him unpredictable. Lanny calls him a "thrill seeker", due to his living in the jungle, and he's shown to being rather calm or oblivious during different dangerous situations. He likes spending time in the jungle, and has a knack for surviving in the wild; claiming "the only thing you need is a trusty pocket knife", and appropriately is known for his quick-thinking when in danger. Boz seems to be a little bit flirty, he hits on girls often throughout the episodes, even with little success. Despite that, he still "pathetically" likes his ex-girlfriend Sasha, even though he's actually very upset toward her for ripping his heart out. He goes so far as claiming that he dislikes Mikayla because she "looks, smell and sounds like" Sasha. He's shown in later episodes that he actually likes Mikayla just fine, going along with Boomer's ideas of helping her while repeatedly saying he doesn't like her. Boz's personality actually seems to be a mixture of his brothers' personalities:

characters.name: Brady Parker
characters.description: Alive King of Kinkow (formerly) Human Unlike Boomer, Brady inherited his father's skin. He says he's older, claiming to have "super-fetus memory", but no one knows for sure. Although, they did come close inJourney to the Center of Mt. Spewwhen Boomer wished there was someone who could tell him who was older. Brady is the most mature of the triplets and somewhat braver as shown inBig Mama Waka. In generally situations, he is more serious than Boomer and Boz. He is more intelligent than his brothers, showing a sparkling and infinitely intelligence and his heart is almost always in the right place. InThe Brady Hunchhe went after Boomer, believing the rumor that the Flaji were cannibals and not wanting his brother to be eaten. He was wrong in the end, but his intentions were good. InBeach Bully Bingohe challengedHibachito a surf off in the heat of the moment, to show that he was sick and tired of people bullying him and the rest of the islanders. He can also be manipulative. This is shown inNo Rhyme or Treason, when he tricks Boomer into signing the contract which bans poetry on Kinkow. Unlike Boomer and Boz, Brady is more responsible. InDinner for Squonks, he wanted to take over all his work as king. InGood King Hunting, he take responsibility for the hunt and beats Mason. Brady can also be somewhat power crazy, possibly due to years of being bullied. InNo Rhyme or Treason, he banned poetry on Kinkow and would arrested anyone, because he was jealous of how much Boomer and Mikayla were getting along. Brady is also a little more egotistical. InDinner for Squonks, he outright refused to apologize to the Squonk Queen for insulting her until the very end, because he didn't want to damage his pride. Brady is also very kind and compassionate. InTone Deaf Jam, he asked the islanders not to boo Boomer when he was singing because he didn't want him to get his feelings hurt. Of course, in the same episode, he referred to himself as "King Brady, the all powerful" and "King Brady, the powerful and adorable." Brady isn't that strightforward with people. InBig Kings on Campus, when Mikayla said she knew he was doing all the extreme stuff because he wanted to be like Tristan. He lied and told her that she was right, instead for telling her the truth- that he was doing it to impress her. However, inDo Over, she told him she wanted him to be honest with her, he was. Maybe a little too honest. Brady is a caring and genuine person at heart. InHow I Met Your Brother, he tried to reunite Mason andJasonas a romantic gesture to Mikayla and to show Mason how cool it was to have a brother. When trouble arises, he's always willing to take one for the team. InKings of Legend: Part 2, he never gave up on he and Boomer being the Kings of Legend, even after they gave up the throne to Lanny and he put himself in the line of Zadoc's fire as a distraction, so Boomer could push him back into the Light Side. He also almost always has Boomer's back. InAn Ice Girl for Boomer, he played

characters.name: Candis
characters.description: Candis is a popular girl onKinkow. She is a friend ofMikayla. Candis auditioned to becomeMikayla's best friend. Because of her being obsessed with her look she got Mikayla and herself in danger.(The One About Mikayla's Friends) She later received her ownKinkowgossip show. She helped Mikayla with her 'Bradydreams'. (The Evil King)

characters.name: Chewy
characters.description: Chewy, wasBoomerandBrady'sdog when they lived in Chicago. He only appeared in a narrative flashback in the episodeBeach Party Maggot Massacre. Boomer and Brady were having a big party. While Boomer, Brady, and the guests were dancing, Chewy got into the "Extra Spicy Party Mix," which caused him to puke into a fan. Chewy's puke flew everywhere; which led to the guests referring to the party as "The Barf of July Fiesta."

characters.name: Dirt Fairy
characters.description: Dirt Fairiesare a Tribe/Race onKinkow. The first Dirt Fairy was seen inThe Cheat Life of Brady and Boomerwhere he helpedBoomerandBradycheat. In the episodeMake Dirt, Not War, it is revealed that theFlagiand Dirt Fairies are separated.

characters.name: Dirt Fairy
characters.description: Dirt Fairiesare a Tribe/Race onKinkow. The first Dirt Fairy was seen inThe Cheat Life of Brady and Boomerwhere he helpedBoomerandBradycheat. In the episodeMake Dirt, Not War, it is revealed that theFlagiand Dirt Fairies are separated.

characters.name: Farhog the Fierce
characters.description: "Kinkow belongs to Farhog the Fierce!" - Farhog the Fierce  Farhog was shown to be ruthless, cruel, barbaric, greedy and fearsome. He ﻿strived to conquerKinkow, and would kill anyone who stood in his path. He was also shown to be quite arrogant, as he underestimated Brady in combat which lead to his downfall.

characters.name: Giants
characters.description: Giantsare creatures with Human features but supernatural height and strength. There are many Giants inKinkow. Atog, a common Kinkow Giant. Some of these Giants are,Atog the giantandThe Ogre.

characters.name: Jason Makoola
characters.description: Unknown  Jason is the polar opposite of his brother. He is irresponsible, he cannot think straight and cannot seem to do anything right. UnlikeMason, he is fun and relaxed about life. Jason never has a "Plan B" and makes silly mistakes. He often has poorly thought-of ideas such as selling hot chocolate/coffee at a beach. His brother cares for him but it is because of his rash, reckless and drastic way of thinking that Mason does not want anything to do with him because Mason feels that Jason is a lost cause. Mason had been trying to guide Jason and get him to learn to think ahead and rationally, but to no avail. He got frustrated with him and cut all ties with him. Even though Mason says that Jason is a lost cause, Jason shows some kind of initiative. He comes up with new ways to improve the defences ofThe Castle(although not very good ones). AlthoughMasonwas angered when Jason leftThe Castleopen for attack, he was genuinely trying to do something good. This is why he was so hurt when Mason couldn't see that he had tried to help. Jason actually admires and looks up to his brother which is why he was so upset and hurt when Mason would not give him the benefit of the doubt. He also shows to have an easy bruising self-esteem losing faith in himself after he messed up his security guard job.Mikaylawas the only person who had more faith in him than himself. He cares for Mikayla and bought her a birthday present (which he broke). He is shown to be absent-minded but creative and spontaneous. He has also been shown to be oblivious to circumstances, saying that Mikayla should really be careful not realizing that he was the one who was injuring Mikayla's feet. Like his niece and brother, he is extremely strong. Eventually, he rekindles his relationship with his brother.

Eventually, he rekindles his relationship with his brother.
characters.personality_section: Jason is the polar opposite of his brother. He is irresponsible, he cannot think straight and cannot seem to do anything right. UnlikeMason, he is fun and relaxed about life. Jason never has a "Plan B" and makes silly mistakes. He often has poorly thought-of ideas such as selling hot chocolate/coffee at a beach. His brother cares for him but it is because of his rash, reckless and drastic way of thinking that Mason does not want anything to do with him because Mason feels that Jason is a lost cause. Mason had been trying to guide Jason and get him to learn to think ahead and rationally, but to no avail. He got frustrated with him and cut all ties with him. Even though Mason says that Jason is a lost cause, Jason shows some kind of initiative. He comes up with new ways to improve the defences ofThe Castle(although not very good ones). AlthoughMasonwas angered when Jason leftThe Castleopen for attack, he was genuinely trying to do something good. This is why he was so hurt when Mason couldn't see that he had tried to help. Jason actually admires and looks up to his brother which is why he was so upset and hurt when Mason would not give him the benefit of the doubt. He also shows to have an easy bruising self-esteem losing faith in himself after he messed up his security guard job.Mikaylawas the only person who had more faith in him than himself. He cares for Mikayla and bought her a birthday present (which he broke). He is shown to be absent-minded but creative and spontaneous.

characters.name: Kaita The Bat-Rider
characters.description: "I am Kaita! Master of Darkness and Destroyer of Kings!" - Kaita to King Boz and Boomer Unknown Katia is a feared Kinkowan warrior and villain, tied withZadocandKing Kalakai(in human form). He is shown to be extremely arrogant, greedy, obsessed with his goals and very ruthless, cruel, and barbaric to his pet and to his minions, despite their loyal service to him, as he seemly destroyed/killed the rest of his tribe theTarantula Peopleby turning them all back into tarantulas because they took 10,000 years to resurrect him. He is arguably the darkest villains of the show, as he killedKing Kunu, and presumably, Queen Annabella was also killed by Kaita. This makes Kaita did whatZadocand King Kalakai couldn't; he actually killed a king. Zadoc lost to a king, Kalakai never saw his brotherMalakaiafter being turned into a fish, but Kaita actually murdered King Kunu. This makes him the only villain on the series to have actually succeeded in killing a king. Despite being a dark villain he has several comedy moments as when Boomer and Boz kelp correcting him he would repeat what he says.

characters.name: Lanny Parker
characters.description: September 11th, 1997 Alive Human Lanny is sadistic, cruel, unscrupulous, manipulative, very finicky and power-hungry. He attempts to kill his own cousins on a regular basis so he can become king. He is arrogant and believes himself superior to "greedy peasants" as he calls them. None of the "greedy peasants" like him, they know he is mean. His best and only friend is his talking fish Yamakoshi. Even so, they frequently argue. He appears to have had a change of heart in the last episode. Unlike Kings Brady and Boomer who do let their power go to their heads but have the interests of Kinkow at heart, Lanny is motivated purely by self-interests and abuses his title as king, an example of this would be in the first episode where he did become king when the island believed Boomer and Brady were dead Lanny either imprisoned or killed the people he didn't like. Although he is no physical match, Lanny more than makes up for these shortcomings with his meticulous and crafty schemes. He also had the workings of somewhat of a sociopath, a lack of empathy, an ability to manipulate people with ease and egomania.
